content: How does the author identify himself in the opening of 2 John?
explanation: 2 John 1:1 — 'The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth...' The author (traditionally the apostle John) calls himself 'the elder' — a humble title denoting his role as spiritual overseer.

content: To whom is 2 John addressed?
explanation: 2 John 1:1 — '...to the elect lady and her children...' The recipient may be a specific woman or a figurative reference to a local church and its members.
options: The elect lady and her children

content: According to 2 John 1:1-2, what is the basis for the elder's love for the recipients?
explanation: 2 John 1:1-2 — '...whom I love in truth, and not only I, but also all who know the truth, because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever.' Christian love flows from truth dwelling in the heart.
options: The truth that abides in us and will be with us forever

content: What did the elder rejoice greatly to find among the elect lady's children?
explanation: 2 John 1:4 — 'I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we were commanded by the Father.'
options: That they built a new meeting place
That they sent gifts to the elder
That they were walking in the truth

content: What is the commandment the elder reminds the elect lady of in 2 John 1:5?
explanation: 2 John 1:5 — '...I ask you, dear lady — not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning — that we love one another.'

content: Does the elder say the commandment to love one another is new or already given?
explanation: 2 John 1:5 — '...not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning.' He insists he is only repeating what was delivered from the start — no novel teaching.
options: One we have had from the beginning
A private revelation to the elder
A charge only for the recipient

content: How is love defined in 2 John 1:6?
explanation: 2 John 1:6 — 'And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments.' John defines love by practical obedience, not mere sentiment.
options: That we walk according to His commandments

content: According to 2 John 1:7, what do the deceivers refuse to confess?
explanation: 2 John 1:7 — 'For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh. Such a one is the deceiver and the antichrist.' Denying the Incarnation is the mark of false teaching.
options: That Scripture is the Word of God
That Jesus Christ has come in the flesh

content: What does 2 John 1:7 call such a deceiver?
explanation: 2 John 1:7 — '...Such a one is the deceiver and the antichrist.' The term 'antichrist' here applies to anyone who denies the Incarnation of Christ.

content: How does 2 John conclude — greetings come from whom?
explanation: 2 John 1:13 — 'The children of your elect sister greet you.' The closing affirms the communal nature of the church — brothers and sisters in faith greet one another.
options: The children of your elect sister

content: In 2 John 1:8, the elder warns the recipients to watch themselves so they may not lose what?
explanation: 2 John 1:8 — 'Watch yourselves, so that you may not lose what we have worked for, but may win a full reward.' This warns that falling into false teaching may destroy the fruit already gained.
options: What they have worked for and a full reward

content: According to 2 John 1:9, who does NOT have God?
explanation: 2 John 1:9 — 'Everyone who goes on ahead and does not abide in the teaching of Christ, does not have God.' 'Going on ahead' describes those who claim advancement beyond delivered doctrine — a form of heresy.
options: The one who does not read Scripture daily
The one who does not tithe
Everyone who goes on ahead and does not abide in the teaching of Christ

content: According to 2 John 1:9, whoever abides in the teaching of Christ has what?
explanation: 2 John 1:9 — 'Whoever abides in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.' Right doctrine about Christ leads to true fellowship with the Triune God.
options: Both the Father and the Son
Only the privilege of answered prayer
A position as elder in the church

content: According to 2 John 1:10, what should believers do if someone comes without the teaching of Christ?
explanation: 2 John 1:10 — '...do not receive him into your house or give him any greeting.' This guarded the early church from false teachers exploiting hospitality to spread error.
options: Debate Scripture with them to the end
Not receive them into the house and give no greeting
Invite them to preach once as a test
Pray over them openly before their face

content: Why is greeting a false teacher so serious according to 2 John 1:11?
explanation: 2 John 1:11 — 'For whoever greets him takes part in his wicked works.' Public welcome implies endorsement — and thus partnership in evil deeds.
options: It wastes time meant for prayer
The greeter takes part in the wicked works
It offends the elders of the church

content: Why does the elder not wish to write more with paper and ink (2 John 1:12)?
explanation: 2 John 1:12 — 'Though I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink. Instead I hope to come to you and talk face to face, so that our joy may be complete.'
options: Paper and ink were too expensive
He feared enemies might read it
He wanted to keep revelation secret
He hoped to talk face to face so joy might be complete

content: According to 2 John 1:3, from whom do 'grace, mercy, and peace' come? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 John 1:3 — 'Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us, from God the Father and from Jesus Christ the Father's Son, in truth and love.' Only two sources are named — the Father and the Son.

content: According to 2 John 1:2, how will the truth that abides in us continue?
explanation: 2 John 1:2 — '...because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever.' Truth is not a passing knowledge but an eternal reality in the believer's life.
options: It will be with us forever

content: According to 2 John 1:10-11, what should believers NOT do with someone who comes without the teaching of Christ? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 John 1:10-11 — 'Do not receive him into your house or give him any greeting, for whoever greets him takes part in his wicked works.' The text names only these two specific prohibitions — hosting and formal greeting.
options: Pray for him silently in the heart

content: According to 2 John 1:7, which characteristics describe the 'deceivers'? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 John 1:7 — 'Many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh. Such a one is the deceiver and the antichrist.' Three marks: gone into the world, deny the incarnation, and are the antichrist. The verse does not speak of resurrection.
options: They have gone out into the world
They do not confess Jesus Christ coming in the flesh
